title: National Bank of Pakistan

infobox.value: * Rehmat Ali Hasnie ([CEO](/wiki/CEO "CEO")) * Ashraf Mahmood Wathra ([Chairman](/wiki/Chairman "Chairman"))
Loans, Savings, Consumer Banking, Corporate Banking, Investment Banking
[State Bank of Pakistan](/wiki/State_Bank_of_Pakistan "State Bank of Pakistan") (75.20%)

(**NBP**) is a Pakistani state-owned [multinational](/wiki/Multinational_corporation "Multinational corporation") [commercial bank](/wiki/Commercial_bank "Commercial bank") which is a subsidiary of [State Bank of Pakistan](/wiki/State_Bank_of_Pakistan "State Bank of Pakistan"). It is headquartered in [Karachi, Pakistan](/wiki/Karachi,_Pakistan "Karachi, Pakistan"). As of December 2022, it has over 1,500 [branches](/wiki/Branch_(banking) "Branch (banking)") across [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
["The World's Highest ATM"](https://www.atlasobscura.com/places/worlds-highest-atm). *[Atlas Obscura](/wiki/Atlas_Obscura "Atlas Obscura")*. Retrieved 22 October 2019.

["BANK OF PAKISTAN OPENS BRANCH HERE"](https://www.nytimes.com/1964/09/26/archives/bank-of-pakistan-opens-branch-here.html). *New York Times*. September 26, 1964.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

State-owned commercial bank in Pakistan

National Bank of Pakistan

**National Bank of Pakistan** (**NBP**) is a Pakistani state-owned [multinational](/wiki/Multinational_corporation "Multinational corporation") [commercial bank](/wiki/Commercial_bank "Commercial bank") which is a subsidiary of [State Bank of Pakistan](/wiki/State_Bank_of_Pakistan "State Bank of Pakistan"). It is headquartered in [Karachi, Pakistan](/wiki/Karachi,_Pakistan "Karachi, Pakistan"). As of December 2022, it has over 1,500 [branches](/wiki/Branch_(banking) "Branch (banking)") across [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The bank provides various commercial and public sector banking services, including the debt-equity market, corporate investment banking, [retail](/wiki/Retail "Retail") and [consumer](/wiki/Consumer "Consumer") banking, [agricultural](/wiki/Agriculture "Agriculture") [financing](/wiki/Financing "Financing"), [treasury](/wiki/Treasury "Treasury") services. In the year 2020, the bank was designated domestic [systemically important bank](/wiki/Systemically_important_financial_institution "Systemically important financial institution") (D-SIB) by the [State Bank of Pakistan](/wiki/State_Bank_of_Pakistan "State Bank of Pakistan").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Bank_of_Pakistan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The National Bank of Pakistan (NBP) was established in 1949 under the National Bank of Pakistan Ordinance as a government-owned bank. Initially, NBP functioned as an agent of the central bank in areas where the [State Bank of Pakistan](/wiki/State_Bank_of_Pakistan "State Bank of Pakistan") did not have branches and managed government treasury operations. The bank's first branches were situated in the jute-growing regions of [East Pakistan](/wiki/East_Pakistan "East Pakistan"), followed by offices in [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi") and [Lahore](/wiki/Lahore "Lahore").

In 1950, NBP expanded internationally by opening a branch in [Jeddah](/wiki/Jeddah "Jeddah"), [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia"). By 1955, the bank had established additional branches in [London](/wiki/London "London") and [Calcutta](/wiki/Calcutta "Calcutta"). Further international expansion included the establishment of a branch in [Baghdad](/wiki/Baghdad "Baghdad"), [Iraq](/wiki/Iraq "Iraq"), in 1957 and in [Dar es Salaam](/wiki/Dar_es_Salaam "Dar es Salaam"), [Tanganyika](/wiki/Tanganyika_(1961%E2%80%931964) "Tanganyika (1961–1964)"), in 1962. In 1964, a branch was opened in the United States.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Geopolitical events impacted NBP's overseas operations during this period. In 1964, the [Iraqi government](/wiki/Iraqi_government "Iraqi government") nationalised NBP's Baghdad branch. The outbreak of hostilities between India and Pakistan in 1965 led the [Indian government](/wiki/Indian_government "Indian government") to seize the Calcutta branch. In 1967, the [Tanzanian government](/wiki/Tanzanian_government "Tanzanian government") nationalised the Dar es Salaam branch.

In 1971, NBP acquired two branches of the [Bank of China](/wiki/Bank_of_China "Bank of China") in [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi") and [Chittagong](/wiki/Chittagong "Chittagong"). The separation of East Pakistan resulted in the loss of NBP's branches in that region. Subsequently, NBP merged with Eastern Mercantile Bank and Eastern Bank Corporation. In 1974, the [government of Pakistan](/wiki/Government_of_Pakistan "Government of Pakistan") nationalised NBP, and as part of the consolidation of the banking sector, NBP acquired the [Bank of Bahawalpur](/wiki/Bank_of_Bahawalpur "Bank of Bahawalpur").

The bank continued its international operations by opening an offshore branch in [Cairo](/wiki/Cairo "Cairo") in 1977. In 1994, NBP amalgamated Mehran Bank, and in 1997, it commenced operations of a branch in [Ashgabat](/wiki/Ashgabat "Ashgabat"), [Turkmenistan](/wiki/Turkmenistan "Turkmenistan").[[6]](#cite_note-6) In 2000, NBP opened a representative office in [Almaty](/wiki/Almaty "Almaty"), [Kazakhstan](/wiki/Kazakhstan "Kazakhstan").

In 2001, the State Bank of Pakistan and the [Bank of England](/wiki/Bank_of_England "Bank of England") permitted only two Pakistani banks to operate in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"). As a result, NBP and [United Bank Limited](/wiki/United_Bank_Limited "United Bank Limited") merged their UK operations to form Pakistan International Bank, with NBP holding a 45% stake and United Bank owning 55%. In 2002, Pakistan International Bank was renamed United National Bank Limited (UNB), maintaining the same ownership structure. Following its [privatisation in Pakistan](/wiki/Privatisation_in_Pakistan "Privatisation in Pakistan"), UNB was owned 49% by the government of Pakistan and 51% by a joint foreign consortium from Abu Dhabi.

During this period, NBP underwent partial privatisation and the government of Pakistan sold 23.2 percent share through [initial public offering](/wiki/Initial_public_offering "Initial public offering") on the [Karachi Stock Exchange](/wiki/Karachi_Stock_Exchange "Karachi Stock Exchange").

In 2003, NBP opened a branch in [Kabul](/wiki/Kabul "Kabul"), [Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan "Afghanistan"), where the first ATM in the country was installed. The offshore branch in Cairo was closed in 2005. In 2010, NBP opened a branch in [Karaganda](/wiki/Karaganda "Karaganda"), [Kazakhstan](/wiki/Kazakhstan "Kazakhstan"), and in 2011, it established a representative office in [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada").

### Subsidiaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Bank_of_Pakistan&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Subsidiaries")]

* Taurus Securities
* NBP Exchange
* NBP Leasing
* NBP Modaraba
* CJSC Bank in Almaty, Kazakhstan
* United National Bank in the UK

* NBP Funds is a Non-Banking Finance Company jointly owned by National Bank of Pakistan and Fullerton Fund Management Group, Singapore, which in-turn is a wholly owned subsidiary of [Temasek Holdings](/wiki/Temasek_Holdings "Temasek Holdings"), [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore").

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Bank_of_Pakistan&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Sports")]

* Cricket

NBP owns a [team](/wiki/National_Bank_of_Pakistan_cricket_team "National Bank of Pakistan cricket team") since 1947 and a [venue](/wiki/National_Bank_of_Pakistan_Sports_Complex "National Bank of Pakistan Sports Complex") since 1998, both named after it and both located in [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi"). In October 2022, NBP got the naming rights of [National Stadium Karachi](/wiki/National_Bank_Cricket_Arena "National Bank Cricket Arena").

* Football

NBP owns a Karachi-based [men's team](/wiki/NBP_F.C. "NBP F.C.") playing in [Pakistan Premier League](/wiki/Pakistan_Premier_League "Pakistan Premier League").
NBP is not involved with any [women's team](/wiki/Women%27s_association_football "Women's association football") in Pakistan, nevertheless, in October 2020, its director, Imam Baloch, publicly lauded [Diya W.F.C.](/wiki/Diya_W.F.C. "Diya W.F.C.")'s actions for spreading football to poor and "backward areas" of [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi") and [Sindh](/wiki/Sindh "Sindh").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

* Hockey

NBP owns a team which won the National Hockey Championship in 2013.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
